codeReview.summary: This rerun fix resolves the prior approval wire-format blocker end-to-end: shared now defines an explicit approval-response payload with requestId and decision, the server emits that payload for approval POSTs, and the wrapper translates it into the JSON-RPC result Codex expects on stdin. The added shared, server, and wrapper tests cover delivery, detached-state ignoring, and the resulting JSONL shape, and I found no blocking defects in this diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The explicit approval-response relay contract is still not recorded in the mission/library docs, so future workers will have to rediscover that approval replies must carry the original JSON-RPC request id plus an accept/decline decision.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton for session 6e6d4767-e022-48ce-88cb-7837ac99ea61 shows repeated WebSearch/curl lookups for Codex approval-response semantics; .factory/library/architecture.md:122-124 only says prompt or approval POSTs become 'valid JSONL', while shared/src/protocol.ts:101-111, server/src/relay-server.ts:659-664, and wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:295-309 now define the concrete requestId/decision contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ts-worker skill's 'return to orchestrator when Rust wrapper protocol changes are required' rule is too rigid for small cross-language protocol-alignment fixes like this one; the worker had to invoke rust-worker in the same session to finish the assigned feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ts-worker/SKILL.md:89 says to return when Rust wrapper protocol changes are required, but the transcript skeleton shows a rust-worker invocation and the handoff.skillFeedback recommends allowing small cross-language protocol fixes to invoke both skills directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS says shared protocol/types should come from the shared package, but the server still hard-codes the new approval-response message type locally; if current build-order constraints make direct shared imports awkward, that limitation is not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/dl/.factory/missions/33afaa22-b81b-48d3-b9b3-131d99d630c7/AGENTS.md:34 says 'Shared types in shared/ package — import from there, don't duplicate'; server/src/relay-server.ts:74 defines APPROVAL_RESPONSE_MESSAGE_TYPE locally even though shared/src/protocol.ts:38 exports the same constant and server/package.json depends on the shared workspace package.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the mission docs, prior failure report, transcript skeleton, and commit 8d657b4112b106d39dd69a34a37b8b5fb6760881. This fix adequately addresses the prior review's approval wire-format blocker by relaying requestId/decision through the server-wrapper boundary and converting it into the Codex approval response JSONL expected on stdin; no blocking issues found.
